description: A Swift 6 / SwiftUI application for Apple Silicon that transforms photographs into photographic mosaics using Metal-accelerated tile matching, Core Image processing, and AVFoundation video export. Port of the classic AndreaMosaic C++/MFC engine to modern Apple platform technologies.
stack: Swift 6 / SwiftUI / Metal / Core Image / AVFoundation

wave_1.title: Wave 1: Foundation Models
wave_1.description: Tile, ImageLoader, Settings, and Color Analysis -- the core data types and image loading pipeline that every subsequent wave depends on.

wave_2.title: Wave 2: Tile Management
wave_2.description: TileCollectionManager, CPU Tile Scorer, and Tile Cache -- managing tile libraries and implementing the baseline color-distance scoring algorithm.

wave_3.title: Wave 3: Engine Core
wave_3.description: MosaicBuilder, Color Adjustment, Metal Tile Scorer, and MosaicPlan -- the heart of the mosaic engine with GPU-accelerated matching and intermediate plan representation.

wave_4.title: Wave 4: Output
wave_4.description: MosaicExporter (JPEG/PNG/TIFF), Build Reports, and WebMosaicExporter -- rendering final output at full resolution with analytics and interactive HTML deep-zoom viewers.

wave_5.title: Wave 5: SwiftUI Interface
wave_5.description: Menu bar, Mosaic Editor, Collection Browser, and Live Preview -- the full document-based SwiftUI application shell with real-time Metal-backed preview.

wave_6.title: Wave 6: Advanced Features
wave_6.description: Patterns (hex, Voronoi, spiral), Masks, Adaptive Tile Splitting, Video Mosaic via AVFoundation, and PSD Layer Export -- extending beyond the original AndreaMosaic feature set.

wave_7.title: Wave 7: iOS Foundation
wave_7.description: Multi-platform SPM restructure, iOS app entry point with adaptive TabView/NavigationSplitView, PhotoProvider protocol, iCloud Photos adapter, and Google Photos REST adapter with OAuth 2.0 PKCE.

wave_8.title: Wave 8: iOS UI
wave_8.description: Photo library browser with provider picker and scope filtering, PHAsset tile import in batches of 50, iOS mosaic builder with PhotosPicker and preset buttons, and PHAsset-to-CGImage bridge via PHImageManager.

wave_9.title: Wave 9: iOS Output
wave_9.description: iOS mosaic preview with Canvas rendering and pinch-zoom (0.25x–5x), gallery view with LazyVGrid thumbnails and MosaicStorage, HEIC export format, and Photos library save via PHAssetCreationRequest.

wave_10.title: Wave 10: iOS Advanced
wave_10.description: iPad adaptive layout with NavigationSplitView sidebar, adaptive grid columns (3 iPhone / 5 iPad / 7 iPad landscape), thermal manager for mobile CPU/GPU tuning, app icons for all iPhone/iPad sizes, and full iOS localization.

media_compatibility.title: Media Format Compatibility
media_compatibility.description: YJMosaic's I/O layer is built entirely on Apple platform frameworks — ImageIO, AVFoundation, and Core Image. This determines which formats are natively supported and which are not.

formats.notes: Primary tile and source image formats. EXIF orientation auto-applied on load. CMYK and Grayscale inputs normalized to sRGB.

HEIC export added in v0.8.0 (IOS-012) for iOS mosaic output using UTType(filenameExtension: "heic"). ImageLoader.supportedExtensions on macOS does not include HEIC — source images and tiles must be JPEG/PNG/TIFF. HEIC is Apple-native and requires HEVC codec license; macOS ImageIO can decode HEIC but the extension guard was not opened for tile import.

WebP is not listed in ImageLoader.supportedExtensions. Although macOS 14+ ImageIO can decode WebP via the system codec, the extension guard explicitly rejects it. WebP tile import would require adding 'webp' to supportedExtensions — a one-line change — but has not been prioritized.

VideoExtractor uses AVURLAsset which natively supports MP4, MOV, and M4V containers with H.264 and HEVC tracks. Frames are extracted at configurable intervals (default: 1 frame/sec) with static-scene skipping via pixel-variance threshold. VideoMosaicExporter writes output as H.264/HEVC .mov via AVAssetWriter. Full AVFoundation codec pass-through — no transcoding on read.

WebM (VP8/VP9 video, Vorbis/Opus audio) is not supported by AVFoundation on macOS or iOS. Apple's media stack does not include a VP8/VP9 decoder. Supporting WebM would require bundling libvpx and a container demuxer — a substantial dependency not currently in scope.

technology_mapping.title: Technology Port Mapping
technology_mapping.description: Original C++/MFC components mapped to their Swift/macOS equivalents
